data.title: Florida lawmaker reboots crypto reserve bill after the first one flopped
data.description:
Florida House Rep. Webster Barnaby filed a new crypto reserve bill after the first one failed, but this time it isn’t a Bitcoin-only bill, and stricter compliance measures have been added.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/florida-lawmaker-files-new-crypto-reserve-bill?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3F_q%3D1760675834540%26vfff%3D1760675834&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Florida Proposes Bill to Invest in Digital Assets
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a revised bill has been introduced in the Florida House to permit the state to invest in digital assets, including Bitcoin and crypto ETFs. This follows the withdrawal of an earlier proposal by the operations subcommittee in June. The new legislation, known as Florida House Bill 183, was presented by lawmaker Webster Barnaby. It aims to allow the state and certain public entities to allocate up to 10% of their funds into digital assets such as Bitcoin, crypto exchange-traded products, crypto securities, non-fungible tokens, and other blockchain-based products. This bill builds upon Barnaby's previous attempt, HB 487, which was dismissed in June. The updated proposal introduces new standards for custody, documentation, and fiduciary responsibilities related to holding and lending digital assets. Additionally, it broadens the scope of investible digital assets beyond Bitcoin, offering Florida more flexibility in diversifying its digital asset portfolio if the bill is enacted.

HB 183 is set to take effect on July 1, 2026, and would authorize the State Board of Administration to invest pension and other trust funds in digital assets. During the 2025 legislative session, numerous Bitcoin and digital asset reserve bills were proposed across various state legislatures, but only three were successfully enacted into law. These include bills from Arizona, New Hampshire, and Texas. New Hampshire's HB 302 permits the treasurer to invest up to 5% of public funds in digital assets with market caps exceeding $500 billion, currently limited to Bitcoin. Texas Senate Bill 21 establishes a Bitcoin-only reserve, while Arizona's HB 2749 allows for the creation of a digital asset reserve from unclaimed property.

In addition to HB 183, Barnaby has introduced another bill, HB 175, aimed at easing regulatory requirements for stablecoin issuers in Florida. This bill seeks to clarify that recognized payment stablecoin issuers should not be required to obtain separate licenses or registrations. It mandates that stablecoin issuers be fully collateralized with US dollars or treasuries and conduct a public audit of their reserves at least once a month. Like HB 183, the stablecoin bill is also intended to take effect on July 1, 2026.

In related news, California Governor Gavin Newsom recently signed a law to protect unclaimed crypto from being automatically sold. The California SB 822 ensures that digital assets are preserved in their original form rather than being converted to cash before being transferred to state custody. This law allows crypto account holders to recover their original assets by filing a valid claim with the California State Controller.

data.title: Bithumb will launch IN, DOOD and YB Korean Won trading pairs
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bithumb will launch Infinit (IN), Doodles (DOOD) and Yield Basis (YB) Korean won trading pairs.

data.title: Bubblemaps: A single entity is suspected of controlling half of the high-yielding COAI wallets, with a total profit of $13 million
data.description:
Blockchain analytics platform Bubblemaps published a post on the X platform, stating that a single entity is suspected of controlling half of the highest-yielding COAI wallets, with a total profit of $13 million.
Six months ago, 60 wallets displayed similar operating patterns: all initially funded with one BNB through Binance; the funding occurred around 11:00 AM UTC on March 25th; and all executed thousands of automated trades on the Binance Alpha platform. These 60 wallets are considered high-yielding wallets. Overall, over 50% of the top 100 wallets by COAI trading volume belong to a single entity. While there is no evidence of core team involvement, this behavior and level of automation are uncommon.

data.title: Tether Freezes Over $13 Million USDT on Ethereum and TRON
data.description: According to BlockBeats, MistTrack monitoring has revealed that Tether froze 13.4 million USDT across 22 addresses on the Ethereum and TRON blockchains on October 16. Among these, an address starting with 0xecb had 10.3 million USDT frozen, while an address beginning with TYzDe saw 1.4 million USDT frozen.

data.title: The address "25x leverage short ETH" reduced its short position to $87.06 million an hour ago
data.description: According to on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the "25x leverage short ETH" address reduced its ETH position by 3,615.9 ETH and incurred a loss of $69,000 USD just one hour ago. Currently, its ETH short position has been reduced to $87.06 million USD (22,271.47 ETH), with a floating profit of $390,000 USD. The opening price was $3,926.95 USD, and the liquidation price was $3,964.83 USD, just $55 away from the mark price.

data.title: Bitget has launched U-margin ZEC perpetual contracts with leverage ranging from 1 to 25 times
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bitget has launched U-margined ZEC perpetual contracts, with leverage ranging from 1-25x. Contract trading on BOT will also be available simultaneously.

data.title: The crypto sector fell for three consecutive days, with the DePIN sector leading the decline by more than 7%, and ETH fell below $4,000.
data.description: These data suggest that the September job openings reported by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics will further deteriorate. According to SoSoValue data, crypto markets saw widespread declines across all sectors, with Decrypt leading the 24-hour decline, at 7.18%. Within the sector, Render (RENDER) fell 7.05%, and Filecoin (FIL) dropped 7.58%. Additionally, Bitcoin (BTC) fell 2.19%, falling below $109,000. Ethereum (ETH) dropped 2.14%, falling below $4,000. In other sectors, the PayFi sector fell 3.03% over the past 24 hours, with Monero (XMR) and Telcoin (TEL) falling 6.56% and 9.94%, respectively. The CeFi sector fell 3.08%, with Cronos (CRO) down 8.03%. The Layer 1 sector fell 3.71%, with Sui (SUI) down 7.62%. The Layer 2 sector fell 3.74%, but Zora (ZORA) bucked the trend and rose 18.68%. The DeFi sector fell 3.81%, with Aave (AAVE) down 7.58%. The Meme sector fell 4.43%, with Pump.fun (PUMP) down 11.13%. The crypto sector index, which reflects historical market trends, shows that the ssiDePIN, ssiAI, and ssiGameFi indices fell 6.14%, 5.69%, and 5.44%, respectively.

data.title: Ray Dalio Discusses Bitcoin and Stablecoins in Investment Portfolios
data.description:
According to PANews, Ray Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates, recently shared his insights on Bitcoin and stablecoins in an interview with Caixin. Dalio revealed that he has held a small portion of Bitcoin for many years, viewing it as a diversified asset relative to gold. However, he noted that Bitcoin has its drawbacks, particularly the reluctance of central banks to hold it.

Dalio also commented on stablecoins, stating that they are not ideal for wealth storage. He explained that stablecoins are essentially redeemable for corresponding currencies and do not generate interest, making them less financially advantageous than interest-bearing fiat assets. Despite this, stablecoins offer the benefit of global usability, functioning as a convenient transaction settlement system, which may appeal to those indifferent to earning interest.

Regarding the potential of stablecoins to address U.S. debt issues, Dalio suggested that if stablecoin buyers already hold U.S. debt, it merely shifts the debt from one pocket to another. Whether stablecoins can create new demand for U.S. debt remains to be seen.

data.title: Ethereum Leads Blockchain Developer Growth in 2025
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Electric Capital indicates that over 16,000 new developers joined the Ethereum ecosystem between January and September this year. Ethereum has emerged as the largest community of active developers among all blockchain projects, boasting a total of 31,869 developers.

Solana ranks as the second most popular destination for new developers, with over 11,500 joining its ecosystem. However, a representative from the Solana Foundation cautioned that these figures might be outdated. Solana's developer community currently stands at 17,708.

Bitcoin has also seen significant growth, with nearly 7,500 new developers joining its ranks, bringing its total to 11,036 developers. This data highlights the ongoing expansion and interest in blockchain development across various platforms.

data.title: Opinion: Leading indicators point to further weakening of the US job market
data.description: The Kobeissi Letter, in a post on the X platform, stated that leading indicators suggest further weakening in the US job market. In September, US job postings on Indeed fell 2% month-over-month, reaching their lowest level since February 2021. Job postings have fallen 36% since May 2022 and are currently only 2.4% above their pre-pandemic level in February 2020. White-collar jobs, excluding healthcare, have seen the largest decline, currently sitting 8% below their pre-pandemic level. Blue-collar job postings have also declined, but remain approximately 12% above their February 2020 level. These data suggest that job openings reported by the US Bureau of Labor Statistics in September will deteriorate further.

data.title: The Uniswap Web App is now live on the Solana network.
data.description: Uniswap Labs announced on the X platform that the Solana network is now available on the Uniswap Web App. Trading is powered by Jupiter, bringing the total to over 100,000. Next steps include exploring cross-chain bridging, cross-chain trading, and Uniswap wallet support.

data.title: Goldman Sachs survey: Institutional investors are optimistic about stocks and gold, with 52% optimistic about the stock market
data.description: The Kobeissi Letter, in a post on the X platform, stated that a Goldman Sachs survey shows that institutional investors remain bullish on stocks and gold. Approximately 25% of investors surveyed said their favorite trade this quarter was "long gold," reaching its highest level for the second consecutive month. This was followed by "long AI" stocks, at approximately 18%. Forty percent of respondents expect the S&P 500 to outperform other major global indices in October. Overall, 52% of respondents expressed optimism about the stock market, the highest level since December 2024. Furthermore, 55% of participants said they were most bullish on companies in the AI ​​infrastructure sector. Meanwhile, their positions in the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks reached their highest level since at least June 2024.

data.title: Today's Fear and Greed Index dropped to 22, indicating "extreme panic"
data.description: According to Alternative data, the cryptocurrency fear and greed index fell to 22 today (from 28 yesterday), indicating extreme market panic. Note: The fear index threshold is 0-100 and includes the following indicators: volatility (25%) + market trading volume (25%) + social media interest (15%) + market research (15%) + Bitcoin's share of the overall market (10%) + Google search terms analysis (10%).

data.title: Ripple leads $1 billion fundraising drive to stockpile more of its XRP tokens
data.description:
Ripple Labs is raising $1 billion to fill up a new crypto war chest with more XRP, even as most of the market is still wrecked from last week’s meltdown. The company is creating what it calls a digital-asset treasury, or DAT, which will be stacked with XRP. The money will come through a SPAC […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ripple-leads-1-billion-fundraising-for-xrp/

data.title: BTC Position Closure Results in Significant Losses
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, HyperInsight monitoring revealed that WLFI advisor ogle (0x70F) closed a long position of 140 BTC, established four days prior, at 11 PM UTC+8 yesterday, resulting in an actual loss of $890,000. The weekly loss has further expanded to $2.65 million, leaving the account balance below $90,000.

Notably, the opening average price of the BTC position was $115,400, with a liquidation price of $109,700. If the stop-loss had not been executed in time, the position would have been forcibly liquidated. Previous reports indicated that during the '1011' event, the address had already experienced liquidation on ASTER and AVAX long positions.

data.title: DoraHacks officially opens DevRel AI to the public: automating the entire developer relations lifecycle
data.description: DoraHacks, a hackathon platform, has officially announced the public release of DevRel AI (BUIDL AI version 4.0). Organizations can subscribe and activate the service directly on the official website. DevRel AI focuses on developer relations automation, helping organizations set clear goals, track project progress, and effectively manage BUIDL and developer communities. This tool aims to improve developer relations efficiency, extend the lifecycle of hackathons, and advance the transition from event operations to sustainable ecosystem development.
